rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/21/2022 to be heard by Judge Quinn. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Attorney must give notice of the Amended petition to all required parties on the mandatory judicial council form DE-120 including the request for speical notice. Notice cannot be given prior to the petition being filed and it appears the notice filed 2/22/2022 does not include the accurate title of the filed petition. 2) Please explain the starting date for the account. Page 1 does not state. Page 2 line 8 alleges it starts October 31, 2015. Page 5 line 6 alleges it begins November 1, 2015. The GC-400 alleges the account begins November 1, 2015. Property on Hand at start of account period alleges it begins October 31, 2015. 3) Please explain and provide the legal basis for the judicial council form GC-400 property on hand at beginning of account not matching the prior account property on hand at the end of the account. 4) Please explain and provide the legal basis for why First Rep. Account #2699 the ending balances of the prior account does not match the starting balances of this account. 5) Please explain the legal costs reimbursement for 5/26/20216. It appears $670.80 was paid to the attorney but receipts show only $573.30 in expenses. Attorney Vicken Sonentz appeared via courtcall for the 5/10/2016 hearing. Please explain why the estate should pay the costs of a roundtrip ticket and hotel reservation for the attorney when the attorney appeared via courtcall. 6) Please explain the legal costs reimbursed for 10/21/2016 and file the missing receipts. $200.21 was alleged for lodging no receipt filed and counsel appeared via court call. Please explain why the estate should pay the cost of lodging when the attorney appeared via courtcall. It is alleged the court call was $116.00 but the provided court call confirmation page which is not a receipt alleged the statewide fee for court call appears is $86.00.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MYRIAM W. OLIVEN U/D MAY 29,2009 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the legal basis for the property on hand at beginning of the account being $1,402,227.17. The I&A's filed appear to total $1,470,230.17. There is no furniture and furnishings of $1,997.00 on any of the filed I&A' even though it is listed as assets in the account please explain. It appears the account or the I&A's must be amended. 2) Please explain and file a receipt for entry 8/4/2021 on schedule c: disbursements Dixie Miner - Care Management through 7/12/21. 3) The trustee timesheets do not comply with LRSF 14.52. Please file timesheets that have individual entries with the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. Not entries that are grouped by month. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/27/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/25/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Courtesy copies of late filed papers were received on 5/16/22, not by 5/12/22. Nevertheless, they were skimmed in hopes of not continuing the hearing again. Although the amendment was file stamped 5/5/22, due to the Court's staff shortage, it was not actually e-filed (made available for viewing) until 5/9/22, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. The amendment, furthermore, was otherwise identical to the "supplemental declaration" filed on 3/14/22, when some things had changed since then, such as the filing of the Partial #3 I&A, and the information that was provided in yet another "Declaration" filed on 5/10/22 (e-filed 5/11/22) was not incorporated into the amendment. The Arch Plumbing invoice shows the $15,000 received from Mark McNee, not this executor, without any explanation. The property where the work was performed is not identified on that invoice. The rather random invoices for moving and storage fees attached to the "Declaration" do not appear to support the amounts shown on pp. 18-19 of the amended accounting. The bank statement is for the period ending 12/11/20, not 12/31/20. The verification of the "Declaration in Response," etc. refers to the "Supplement". Executor still has not been able to identify the source of unidentified deposits totaling $12,835.18 that were received less than one month before he was issued Letters. (Itemized attorney costs should show the DATE, amount, payee, and description.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/15/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ILENE BRETHOLZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/11/22. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The total of the I&As shown in paragraph 6 of the petition is slightly off. That total does not agree with the Property on Hand at Beginning of the accounting. The Summary of Account shown in paragraph 17 also does not balance. It appears there were other assets not inventoried? Liabilities should be shown on a separate schedule, not as a credit. Transfers between bank accounts are not changes in the form of assets: cash is cash. [Probate Code Sect. 1063(b).] Changes in the number of shares of a fund or security are, however, changes that should be reported on an informational schedule. For example, there were 2,221.553 shares of Putnam Fund appraised at $53,606.07, but 2,445.875 shares were sold having a carry value of $59,333.27. There were 232.981 shares of Fidelity Fund appraised at $10,780.03, but 253.488 shares sold having the same carry value (?). CDs are essentially cash, earning interest; they do not ordinarily get sold for a gain..? Refunds (or "redeposits") of funds paid during estate administration are not "receipts" includable in the fee base. On the other hand, gains on sales are includable in the fee base. Who was paid to prepare the accounting and paid a final payment of $1,237.50? How much was that person paid altogether? A personal representative's ordinary services include the preparation of an accounting; payments to another must be deducted from her statutory compensation. The attorney was also apparently already paid a $1,000 "retainer" for which the administrator seeks reimbursement. Interest shown in one lump sum for the year 2020 is not DETAILED. The "liabilities" on Schedule F must show the original payee. (Receipts and disbursements schedules should always have four columns to show the date, source or PAYEE, description, and amount. [Probate Code Sect. 1062.] There were clearly independent actions taken, as the administrator sold securities. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has PREVIOUSLY been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The estate is not yet ready to close if the jewelry will be sold. An amendment to the accounting and petition must be filed after the jewelry is sold. Notice of the amendment must be given after the amendment is filed. Courtesy copies of the amendment and proof of service and a revised proposed order must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 6/29/22. If the amendment cannot be filed in time to give notice, Counsel should contact the examiner to request a further continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CLARENCE JOSEPH SUGHROUE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Authorize A Proposed Action
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Conservator is requesting to order granting the conservator the authority to increase the line of credit after receiving quotes from no fewer than two reverse mortgage facilities. Court to determine how to proceed and whether to have counsel file an amended petition with actual terms not a blanket order to seek quotes and pick the most favorable. 2) Court appointed attorney Judy Hitchcock has not filed anything regarding this petition. The court would like to hear from the court appointed attorney. 3) The proposed order does not match the prayer in the petition. The petition requests an order granting the conservator the authority to increase the line of credit after receiving quotes from no fewer than two reverse mortgage facilities. The proposed order says the conservator of the estate is ordered to seek no fewer than two quotes from loan companies that regularly underwrite and fund reverse mortgages. The conservator of the estate is ordered to choose among the quotes the one most favorable to the estate of the conservatee. The conservator of the estate is ordered to evaluate the adequacy of the bond after the loan is funded.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIDA QUEBRAL ORQUIZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Leduvina Orquiza and Ely Sonny Orquiza's amended petition: Continued to 7/25/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copy of the amended petition. No proposed order. Incorrect form of Notice. No proof of publication. No Acknowledgement of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities. No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (filed confidentially and conventionally). A cursory review of the amended petition online shows that it is incomplete at item 5, incorrect in item 6 if a sister survived, and incomplete in item 8, as the decedent's sibling who was the parent of any niece or nephew must be listed and his/her date of death must be shown, as required by LRSF 14.18 C.3. Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/15/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT A. THELEEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/27/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must give and file notice of the petition. 2) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 4) Please file a copy of the current vesting deed for the real property and file a copy of the current ownership information for the automobiles. The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. [Evidence Code 662]. 5) Please provide evidence in support of the marriage. [LRSF 14.26] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/13/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED BY AGREEMENT DATED NOVEMBER 22, 1994 BY CHARRA DE MARCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Order Appointing Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: continued to june 27, 2022 to address the following issues: no POS of the NOH on file (30 days notice required); no nominations on filed; no waivers of bond on file; no acceptance of trust from the nominated successor trustee; no copy of the petition and no proposed order delivered to the examiner in Room 202 as required by local rules. finally, no later than noon on june 20, 2022, counsel must cure all of the issues listed above and must also file a supplement to the petition setting forth the background and relevant experience and/or expertise of the proposed successor, explaining why she is an appropriate person to serve. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must file a certified copy of the original will. The court cannot move forward with this petition until a certified copy of the will has been filed. 2) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the requested waiver. 3) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 4) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/27/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
